revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:NIE poster/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:NIE poster/doc]]</includeonly>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Template:NIE poster/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:NIE poster/doc]]</includeonly>

#redirect[[:en:Template:NIE poster/doc]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Template:NIE poster/doc]]</includeonly>

{{Documentation subpage}} === Purpose === To link to articles in [[Wikisource]] editions of the ''[[New International Encyclopedia]]''. === Usage === {{NIE poster}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name|Display Name}} In addition, there is a year parameter which defaults to 1905. === Examples === {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} This result is displayed in the top box to the right. If the NIE article name looks too ugly, use: {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} This result is displayed in the bottom box to the right. If no article name is specified, the PAGENAME is used. === Usage tips === When you put this template on a Wikipedia article, please be kind to the regular Wikipedians who are working on those articles. This should be placed at the bottom of the article, usually the External links section. Sometimes it simply won't fit within the Wikipedia article at the bottom, so you may have to use some judgement: think about using the in-line version, {{tl|Cite NIE}}. If the article is about a Wikisource author, it will usually be better to just put a link in the "Works about" section of the author page in Wikisource using [[Wikisource:Template:NIE Link]]. === See also === * {{tl|Cite NIE}}—use when the article is used as a specific reference * [[Wikipedia:Sister projects]] for full usage information {{Sandbox other|| {{DEFAULTSORT:New International Encyclopedia Poster}} [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[ja:Template:NIE Poster]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} === Purpose === To link to articles in [[Wikisource]] editions of the ''[[:en:New International Encyclopedia|New International Encyclopedia]]''. === Usage === {{NIE poster}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name|Display Name}} In addition, there is a year parameter which defaults to 1905. === Examples === {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} This result is displayed in the top box to the right. If the NIE article name looks too ugly, use: {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} This result is displayed in the bottom box to the right. If no article name is specified, the PAGENAME is used. === Usage tips === When you put this template on a Wikipedia article, please be kind to the regular Wikipedians who are working on those articles. This should be placed at the bottom of the article, usually the External links section. Sometimes it simply won't fit within the Wikipedia article at the bottom, so you may have to use some judgement: think about using the in-line version, {{tl|Cite NIE}}. If the article is about a Wikisource author, it will usually be better to just put a link in the "Works about" section of the author page in Wikisource using [[Wikisource:Template:NIE Link]]. === See also === * {{tl|Cite NIE}}—use when the article is used as a specific reference * [[Wikipedia:Sister projects]] for full usage information {{Sandbox other|| {{DEFAULTSORT:New International Encyclopedia Poster}} [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[ja:Template:NIE Poster]] }}

If no article name is specified, the PAGENAME is used. === Usage tips === When you put this template on a Wikipedia article, please be kind to the regular Wikipedians who are working on those articles. Sometimes it simply won't fit within the Wikipedia article at the bottom, so you may have to use some judgement: think about using the in-line version, {{tl|Cite NIE}}. If the article is about a Wikisource author, it will usually be better to just put a link in the "Works about" section of the author page in Wikisource using [[Wikisource:Template:NIE Link]]. === See also === * {{tl|Cite NIE}}—use when the article is used as a specific reference * [[Wikipedia:Sister projects]] for full usage information {{Sandbox other|| {{DEFAULTSORT:New International Encyclopedia Poster}} [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[ja:Template:NIE Poster]] }}

{{Documentation subpage}} === Purpose === To link to articles in [[:en:Wikisource|Wikisource]] editions of the ''[[:en:New International Encyclopedia|New International Encyclopedia]]''. === Usage === {{NIE poster}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name|Display Name}} In addition, there is a year parameter which defaults to 1905. === Examples === {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} This result is displayed in the top box to the right. If the NIE article name looks too ugly, use: {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} This result is displayed in the bottom box to the right. If no article name is specified, the PAGENAME is used. === Usage tips === When you put this template on a Wikipedia article, please be kind to the regular Wikipedians who are working on those articles. This should be placed at the bottom of the article, usually the External links section. Sometimes it simply won't fit within the Wikipedia article at the bottom, so you may have to use some judgement: think about using the in-line version, {{tl|Cite NIE}}. If the article is about a Wikisource author, it will usually be better to just put a link in the "Works about" section of the author page in Wikisource using [[Wikisource:Template:NIE Link]]. === See also === * {{tl|Cite NIE}}—use when the article is used as a specific reference * [[Wikipedia:Sister projects]] for full usage information {{Sandbox other|| {{citation needed|New International Encyclopedia Poster}} [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[ja:Template:NIE Poster]] }}

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} === Purpose === To link to articles in [[:en:Wikisource|Wikisource]] editions of the ''[[:en:New International Encyclopedia|New International Encyclopedia]]''. === Usage === {{NIE poster}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name|Display Name}} In addition, there is a year parameter which defaults to 1905. === Examples === {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} This result is displayed in the top box to the right. If the NIE article name looks too ugly, use: {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} This result is displayed in the bottom box to the right. If no article name is specified, the PAGENAME is used. === Usage tips === When you put this template on a Wikipedia article, please be kind to the regular Wikipedians who are working on those articles. This should be placed at the bottom of the article, usually the External links section. Sometimes it simply won't fit within the Wikipedia article at the bottom, so you may have to use some judgement: think about using the in-line version, {{tl|Cite NIE}}. If the article is about a Wikisource author, it will usually be better to just put a link in the "Works about" section of the author page in Wikisource using [[Wikisource:Template:NIE Link]]. === See also === * {{tl|Cite NIE}}—use when the article is used as a specific reference * [[Wikipedia:Sister projects]] for full usage information {{Sandbox other|| {{citation needed|New International Encyclopedia Poster}} [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[ja:Template:NIE Poster]] }} {{wikidata link|Q14397262}}

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} === Purpose === To link to articles in [[:en:Wikisource|Wikisource]] editions of the ''[[:en:New International Encyclopedia|New International Encyclopedia]]''. === Usage === {{NIE poster}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name|Display Name}} In addition, there is a year parameter which defaults to 1905. === Examples === {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} This result is displayed in the top box to the right. If the NIE article name looks too ugly, use: {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} This result is displayed in the bottom box to the right. If no article name is specified, the PAGENAME is used. === Usage tips === When you put this template on a Wikipedia article, please be kind to the regular Wikipedians who are working on those articles. This should be placed at the bottom of the article, usually the External links section. Sometimes it simply won't fit within the Wikipedia article at the bottom, so you may have to use some judgement: think about using the in-line version, {{tl|Cite NIE}}. If the article is about a Wikisource author, it will usually be better to just put a link in the "Works about" section of the author page in Wikisource using [[Wikisource:Template:NIE Link]]. === See also === * {{tl|Cite NIE}}—use when the article is used as a specific reference * [[Wikipedia:Sister projects]] for full usage information {{Sandbox other|| {{citation needed|New International Encyclopedia Poster}} [[ja:Template:NIE Poster]] }} {{wikidata link|Q14397262}} [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} === Purpose === To link to articles in [[:en:Wikisource|Wikisource]] editions of the ''[[:en:New International Encyclopedia|New International Encyclopedia]]''. === Usage === {{NIE poster}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name|Display Name}} In addition, there is a year parameter which defaults to 1905. === Examples === {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} This result is displayed in the top box to the right. If the NIE article name looks too ugly, use: {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} This result is displayed in the bottom box to the right. If no article name is specified, the PAGENAME is used. === Usage tips === When you put this template on a Wikipedia article, please be kind to the regular Wikipedians who are working on those articles. This should be placed at the bottom of the article, usually the External links section. Sometimes it simply won't fit within the Wikipedia article at the bottom, so you may have to use some judgement: think about using the in-line version, {{tl|Cite NIE}}. If the article is about a Wikisource author, it will usually be better to just put a link in the "Works about" section of the author page in Wikisource using [[Wikisource:Template:NIE Link]]. === See also === * {{tl|Cite NIE}}—use when the article is used as a specific reference * [[Wikipedia:Sister projects]] for full usage information {{Sandbox other|| {{citation needed|New International Encyclopedia Poster}} [[ja:Template:NIE Poster]] }} {{wikidata link|Q14397262}} [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates]]

Sometimes it simply won't fit within the Wikipedia article at the bottom, so you may have to use some judgement: think about using the in-line version, {{tl|Cite NIE}}. If the NIE article name looks too ugly, use: {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} This result is displayed in the bottom box to the right. If no article name is specified, the PAGENAME is used. === Usage tips === When you put this template on a Wikipedia article, please be kind to the regular Wikipedians who are working on those articles. This should be placed at the bottom of the article, usually the External links section. Sometimes it simply won't fit within the Wikipedia article at the bottom, so you may have to use some judgement: think about using the in-line version, {{tl|Cite NIE}}. If the article is about a Wikisource author, it will usually be better to just put a link in the "Works about" section of the author page in Wikisource using [[Wikisource:Template:NIE Link]]. === See also === * {{tl|Cite NIE}}—use when the article is used as a specific reference * [[Wikipedia:Sister projects]] for full usage information {{Sandbox other|| {{citation needed|New International Encyclopedia Poster}} [[ja:Template:NIE Poster]] }} {{wikidata link|Q14397262}} [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

Sometimes it simply won't fit within the Wikipedia article at the bottom, so you may have to use some judgement: think about using the in-line version, {{tl|Cite NIE}}. revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments; consolidate 1 interlanguage link(s) into wikidata link template [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24951476559|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} === Purpose === To link to articles in [[:en:Wikisource|Wikisource]] editions of the ''[[:en:New International Encyclopedia|New International Encyclopedia]]''. === Usage === {{NIE poster}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name}} or {{NIE poster|Article Name|Display Name}} In addition, there is a year parameter which defaults to 1905. === Examples === {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria}} This result is displayed in the top box to the right. If the NIE article name looks too ugly, use: {{NIE poster|Bœhmeria|Boehmeria}} This result is displayed in the bottom box to the right. If no article name is specified, the PAGENAME is used. === Usage tips === When you put this template on a Wikipedia article, please be kind to the regular Wikipedians who are working on those articles. This should be placed at the bottom of the article, usually the External links section. Sometimes it simply won't fit within the Wikipedia article at the bottom, so you may have to use some judgement: think about using the in-line version, {{tl|Cite NIE}}. If the article is about a Wikisource author, it will usually be better to just put a link in the "Works about" section of the author page in Wikisource using [[Wikisource:Template:NIE Link]]. === See also === * {{tl|Cite NIE}}—use when the article is used as a specific reference * [[Wikipedia:Sister projects]] for full usage information {{Sandbox other|| {{citation needed|New International Encyclopedia Poster}} }} {{wikidata link|Q14397262|ja|Template:NIE Poster}} [[Category:Encyclopedia source templates]] [[Category:Wikisource link templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
